activation_mode.mechanism: Amp rule files load unconditionally; no conditional or model-decision activation documented

auto_memory.mechanism: Amp does not have an AI-generated auto-memory system

cross_provider_recognition.mechanism: Amp reads its own rule format; no cross-provider rule file recognition documented

file_imports.mechanism: No file import syntax documented for Amp rule files

hierarchical_loading.mechanism: Amp rule files are project-scoped; no subdirectory hierarchy documented

providerExtensions.name: Canonical filename: AGENT.md
providerExtensions.description: Amp rules files are named AGENT.md. The filename is fixed — no frontmatter, no directory wrapper. The file contains plain Markdown with behavioral instructions, coding conventions, workflow guidelines, and any other context the agent should follow when working in the project.

providerExtensions.name: Project-scope placement at repository root
providerExtensions.description: AGENT.md is placed at the root of the project repository. It is project-scoped: the instructions apply when Amp operates within that project. The file can be committed to version control so the whole team benefits from the same guidelines.

providerExtensions.name: Plain Markdown, no frontmatter
providerExtensions.description: AGENT.md contains plain Markdown only — no YAML frontmatter, no structured metadata fields. Sections are organized with Markdown headings. Typical content observed includes: build commands and scripts, architecture and directory layout, code style and conventions, testing methodology (e.g., TDD requirements), PR workflow rules, tool and dependency notes, and any team-specific behavioral instructions for the AI.
